# Pull Request

## Description
<!-- Clearly describe what this PR does and why it's needed -->

## Type of Change
- [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
- [ ] 📚 Documentation update
- [ ] 🎨 Style update (formatting, renaming)
- [ ] ♻️ Refactoring (no functional changes, no api changes)
- [ ] 🧪 Test update
- [ ] 🔧 Configuration change
- [ ] 📦 Dependency update

## Checklist
- [ ] My code follows the project's style guidelines
- [ ] I have performed a self-review of my own code
- [ ] I have commented my code, particularly in hard-to-understand areas
- [ ] I have made corresponding changes to the documentation
- [ ] My changes generate no new warnings
- [ ] I have added tests that prove my fix is effective or that my feature works
- [ ] New and existing unit tests pass locally with my changes
- [ ] Any dependent changes have been merged and published in downstream modules

## Testing
<!-- Describe the tests you ran to verify your changes -->

### Test Coverage
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated
- [ ] Integration tests added/updated
- [ ] E2E tests added/updated

## Security Considerations
- [ ] I have considered the security implications of this change
- [ ] No sensitive data is exposed
- [ ] Input validation is implemented where needed
- [ ] Authentication/authorization checks are in place

## Performance Impact
- [ ] I have considered the performance implications of this change
- [ ] No significant performance regression introduced
- [ ] Optimizations implemented where applicable

## Screenshots / Recordings
<!-- If applicable, add screenshots or screen recordings to help explain your changes -->

## Related Issues
<!-- Link to any related issues (e.g., Fixes #123, Related to #456) -->

## Additional Context
<!-- Add any other context about the PR here -->
